title: How I built a zero-dependency static site generator in a weekend
excerpt: Sometimes the best way to understand a tool is to build it yourself. I spent a weekend writing a minimal SSG — no frameworks, no build chains — and learned more about templating than two years of using existing tools.

title: Type-safe API clients without code generation
excerpt: Using TypeScript's type system to infer request and response shapes directly from a schema object, no codegen step needed.

title: Rewriting our CLI tool in Go: what changed, what didn't
excerpt: A six-month retrospective on migrating a Node.js dev tool to Go. Startup time, binary distribution, and the things we didn't anticipate.

title: TIL: TypeScript's satisfies operator and when to use it
excerpt: A small but genuinely useful addition in TS 4.9 that lets you validate a value against a type without widening it.

title: Six months of writing publicly: what I learned
excerpt: An honest look at what's surprised me about maintaining a technical blog — the feedback loops, the drafts that never shipped, and the ones that did.

title: Graceful shutdown patterns in Go HTTP servers
excerpt: How to handle SIGINT and SIGTERM properly so in-flight requests finish before your process exits.
